body.lead_text:
Summer Molecular Science Program for Grades Entering 9-12

+ 3D Molecular Visualization 

+ Computer Simulations 

+ Engineering Applications in Biology and Materials 

+ Investigate Behavior of Polymer chains such as DNA, proteins, & plastics 

+ Molecular Motion & Diffusion 

+ Hydrophobic Interactions & Molecular Organization

events_list.title: Welch Crystallization Symposium 2026
events_list.preview: We are pleased to announce that the annual Welch Crystallization Symposium will take place on October 9, 2026.

body.lead_text:
Hosted by the Welch Center for Advanced Bioactive Materials Crystallization, this event serves as a premier forum for researchers, faculty, and industry professionals. Attendees will explore the latest advancements in crystal nucleation, molecular engineering, and nonclassical crystallization pathways.

Key Information:
Date: Friday, October 9, 2026
Host: Welch Center for Advanced Bioactive Materials Crystallization
Audience: Materials scientists, chemical engineers, chemists, and industry experts

Stay tuned for upcoming announcements regarding our keynote speaker lineup, session topics, and registration details.

body.full_title: Prof. Rimer to speak at TAMEST’s O’Donnell Awards Summit

events_list.preview: Prof. Rimer has been invited to speak at the Edith and Peter O'Donnell Awards Summit hosted by the Texas Academy of Medicine, Science, and Technology.
